prerequisites: A SendGrid API key with at least Read Access to the resources you want to extract (Full Access also works).
A start_date value marking the earliest point from which to pull data.

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the single api_key secret field. The SendGrid v3 API uses bearer-token authentication: each request sends an Authorization header with the value 'Bearer <YOUR_API_KEY>'. Generate the key in the SendGrid app under Settings > API Keys.

setupSteps.title: Create a SendGrid API key
setupSteps.body: In the SendGrid app, go to Settings in the left navigation and select API Keys, then click Create API Key. Give the key a name and choose an access level: Full Access (access across the account except billing) or Custom Access (set per-category permissions such as Read Access for GET-only). Read Access is sufficient for extraction. Click Create & View, then copy the key immediately, because SendGrid shows it only once.

setupSteps.title: Provide the api_key
setupSteps.body: Paste the generated key into the connector's api_key field. The connector sends it as the bearer token in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <api_key>) on every request to the SendGrid v3 API. Store the key securely and never commit it to version control.

setupSteps.title: Set the start_date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which the connector should begin extracting data.
